flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: the stale-state problem is only partially fixed. Step 3 re-runs `coordinator.update(...)` during `onTick`, but it still does not re-run the clip-drag snapping and secondary-ghost logic, so `latestStart`, snap behavior, and multi-drag ghosts can still drift while the container keeps scrolling with a stationary pointer.
flags.evidence: In the repo, `useClipDrag` does more than call `coordinator.update(...)` on move: it derives `snappedStart` via `snapDrag(...)`, stores it in `session.latestStart` / `actionDragStateRef`, and recomputes secondary ghosts after the coordinator result (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:273-330). The revised plan's Step 3 `onTick` callback only shows `coordinatorRef.current.update(...)`, so it would refresh the drop indicator but not the rest of the drag state that currently lives below that call path. The original FLAG-001 covered stale snap/drop calculations during stationary-edge scrolling, and that part is still open for clip drags.

flags.concern: Timeline external drop: Over-engineering: Step 4 still assumes `coordinator.editAreaRef` is available inside `useExternalDrop`, but the actual `DragCoordinator` object does not expose that ref and the hook already receives the wrapper element on every `dragover`.
flags.evidence: `DragCoordinator` only exposes `update`, `showSecondaryGhosts`, `end`, and `lastPosition` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useDragCoordinator.ts:15-27). `useExternalDrop` receives only `coordinator: DragCoordinator` in its args (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:12-27), so `coordinator.editAreaRef` is not a real API. At the same time, the hook already gets the timeline wrapper as `event.currentTarget` in `onTimelineDragOver`, and existing repo code resolves `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` from a wrapper when computing drop position (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:74-106; /Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:50-65).

flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll: Scope creep: Step 5 adds a generalized `insets` API and changes left-edge semantics before the repository shows a real gutter bug.
flags.evidence: The current DOM intentionally keeps the label gutter inside the same scroll container: sticky track labels live in each row (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:149-152), the edit area itself is the overflow container (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:494-497), and the gutter is reinforced with a sticky `::before` pseudo-element (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:1-12). The repo already models content offset with `LABEL_WIDTH`/`TIMELINE_START_LEFT` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:4-5). The user request was scrolling near any edge; introducing a generic inset system now expands scope and may change expected left-edge behavior without evidence.
